data.title: Second Quarter 2025 - Earnings Release
data.description:
ALBEMARLE, N.C., July 25, 2025 /PRNewswire/ — Uwharrie Capital Corp and its subsidiary, Uwharrie Bank (the "Bank"), reported consolidated total assets of $1.17 billion at June 30, 2025, versus $1.13 billion at December 31, 2024.

Net income for the six-month period ended June 30, 2025, was $5.4 million versus $4.6 million for the same period in 2024. For the six months ended June 30, 2025, net income available to common shareholders was $5.1 million, or $0.72 per share, compared to $4.3 million, or $0.60 per share, for the six months ended June 30, 2024. Net income available to common shareholders takes into consideration the payment of dividends on preferred stock issued by the Bank.

Net income for the three-month period ended June 30, 2025, was $2.8 million versus $2.2 million for the same period in 2024. For the three months ended June 30, 2025, net income available to common shareholders was $2.7 million, or $0.38 per share, compared to $2.1 million, or $0.29 per share, for the three months ended June 30, 2024.

About Uwharrie Capital Corp

Uwharrie Capital Corp offers a full range of financial solutions through its subsidiaries: Uwharrie Bank and Uwharrie Investment Advisors. Additional information on Uwharrie Capital Corp may be found at www.Uwharrie.com or by calling 704-982-4415.

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/second-quarter-2025---earnings-release-302514128.html

SOURCE UWHARRIE CAPITAL CORP

data.title: US, China confront each other on Ukraine at United Nations
data.description:
The United States told China at the United Nations on Friday it should "stop fueling Russia's aggression" in Ukraine, as China accused Washington of trying to shift blame and spark confrontation.

Acting U.S. Ambassador to the U.N. Dorothy Shea urged all countries, specifically naming China, to stop exports to Russia of dual-use goods that Washington says contribute to Russia’s war industrial base and enable its drone and missile attacks on Ukraine.

“Beijing’s claim to have implemented strong export controls on dual-use goods falls apart in the face of daily recovery of Chinese-produced components in the drones, weapons, and vehicles that Russia uses against Ukraine,” Shea told a meeting of the 15-member U.N. Security Council on Ukraine.

China did not start the war in Ukraine, is not a party to the conflict, has never provided lethal weapons, and has always “strictly controlled dual-use materials, including the export of drones,” China's deputy U.N. Ambassador Geng Shuang responded.

“We urge the U.S. to stop shifting blame on the Ukraine issue or creating confrontation and instead play a more constructive role in promoting ceasefire and peace talks,” he told the council.

Reuters reported on Wednesday that Chinese-made engines are being covertly shipped via front companies to a state-owned drone manufacturer in Russia, labeled as "industrial refrigeration units" to avoid detection in the wake of Western sanctions.

"If China is sincere in calling for peace, it should stop fueling Russia's aggression," Shea said.

data.title: Nvidia AI Chip Repairs Soar In China
data.description:
Nvidia chips keep China's repair shops busy despite U.S. export bans.

Reuters reports that about a dozen boutique firms in Shenzhen have quietly built businesses fixing advanced Nvidia AI chipsH100s, A100s and morethat shouldn't even be in the country.

One repair shop spun off a new unit late last year and now handles up to 500 GPUs a month, underscoring really significant repair demand, says a co?owner who's been tuning Nvidia gaming cards for 15 years.

Meanwhile the Financial Times notes that nearly $1 billion of Nvidia AI chips still flowed into China in the three months after tighter U.S. export curbs, highlighting gaps in enforcement and the lengths firms will go to fuel AI projects.

Analysts say these repair services not only keep existing hardware alive but also chip away at the impact of export restrictions meant to slow China's AI push.

data.title: FB Financial Corporation Declares Regular Quarterly Dividend
data.description:
NASHVILLE, Tenn.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--July 25, 2025--

FB Financial Corporation announced today that its board of directors declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.19 per share. The dividend is payable on August 26, 2025, to shareholders of record as of August 12, 2025.

"Our commitment to providing consistent shareholder returns is a core element of our capital management strategy," stated Christopher T. Holmes, President and Chief Executive Officer. "Achieving this 30th consecutive quarterly dividend is a direct reflection of that commitment and FB Financial's ability to deliver shareholder value while maintaining a strong capital position to pursue future growth opportunities."

ABOUT FB FINANCIAL CORPORATION

FB Financial Corporation is a financial holding company headquartered in Nashville, Tennessee. FB Financial Corporation operates through its wholly owned banking subsidiary, FirstBank in Tennessee, Kentucky, Alabama and Georgia. FB Financial Corporation operates 93 full-service branches across its footprint and has approximately $16.0 billion in total assets.

data.title: Did You Suffer Losses in Fiserv, Inc. (FI)? Contact Levi & Korsinsky About Securities Fraud Claims
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / July 25, 2025 / Levi & Korsinsky notifies investors that it has commenced an investigation of Fiserv, Inc. ("Fiserv, Inc.") concerning possible violations of federal securities laws.

On July 23, 2025, before market-open, Fiserv published second quarter 2025 financial results and hosted an earnings call. As part of the Company's press release, Fiserv disclosed that it revised its 2025 outlook from a previous range of 10-12% refined organic revenue growth outlook to 10%. CEO Lyons pointed to delayed Company launches and initiatives for the revised 2025 guidance.

Following this news, Fiserv's stock price fell over 17% to open at $137.00 per share on July 23, 2025. To obtain additional information, go to:

data.title: Entergy quarterly dividend payment to shareholders
data.description:
NEW ORLEANS, July 25, 2025 /PRNewswire/ — Entergy's board of directors today declared a quarterly dividend payment of $0.60 per share on the company's common stock. The dividend is payable Sept. 2, 2025, to shareholders of record as of Aug. 13, 2025.

Entergy has paid shareholders a cash dividend on its common stock continuously since 1988.

About Entergy

Entergy is a Fortune 500 company that powers life for 3 million customers through our operating companies in Arkansas, Louisiana, Mississippi and Texas. We're investing in the reliability, resilience and growth of the energy system while helping our region transition to cleaner, more efficient energy solutions. With roots in our communities for more than 100 years, Entergy is a nationally recognized leader in sustainability and corporate citizenship. Since 2018, we have delivered more than $100 million in economic benefits each year to local communities through philanthropy, volunteerism and advocacy. Entergy is headquartered in New Orleans, Louisiana, and has approximately 12,000 employees. Learn more at entergy.com and connect with @Entergy on social media. #WePowerLife

Levi & Korsinsky Investigates Replimune Group, Inc. (REPL) Over Possible Securities Fraud
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / July 25, 2025 / Levi & Korsinsky notifies investors that it has commenced an investigation of Replimune Group, Inc. ("Replimune Group, Inc.") concerning possible violations of federal securities laws.

On July 22, 2025, Replimune announced that it received a Complete Response Letter from the FDA rejecting its Biologics License Application for RP1 (vusolimogene oderparepvec) in combination with nivolumab for the treatment of advanced melanoma. In the CRL, the FDA indicated that "the IGNYTE trial is not considered to be an adequate and well-controlled clinical investigation that provides substantial evidence of effectiveness."

Following this news, REPL's stock price fell over 78% to open at $2.70 per share on July 22, 2025.

Genworth Shares Rise On UK Court Ruling That Could Lead to $750 Million Windfall
data.description:
By Nicholas G. Miller

Shares of Genworth Financial rose Friday after a U.K. court ruling in a payment protection insurance case that could lead to a $750 million windfall for the company.

The stock of the Richmond, Va.-based financial services company was up 7.9% to $7.92 and has risen 13% so far this year.

The ruling found the bank Santander liable for losses incurred by financial services company AXA resulting from Santander's mis-selling of payment protection insurance policies, Genworth said.

The policies were underwritten by two companies that AXA acquired from Genworth in 2015. Under previous agreements between the two companies, Genworth is entitled to a portion of the money that AXA recovers related to the mis-selling losses. That potentially entitles Genworth to $750 million, the company said.

Genworth said it would use the funds for investments, share buybacks and debt repayment.

Write to Nicholas G. Miller at nicholas.miller@wsj.com.

data.title: For Sale: A $35 Million Historic Home Near Harvard — WSJ
data.description:
By Sarah Paynter

A circa-1870s house near Harvard University is asking $35 million, making it one of the most expensive homes for sale in the Boston area.

Sitting on 1.23 acres with a pool, the Cambridge, Mass., home is owned by early Lululemon Athletica investor RoAnn Costin, a Harvard graduate. In 1983, Costin and her ex-husband paid about $450,000 for the house near Harvard Square, then spent about $6 million adding to the estate over the next few decades. The resulting property has more acreage than most Cambridge homes, according to Gail Roberts of Coldwell Banker Realty, who has the listing with colleague Ed Feijo.

The roughly 8,200-square-foot brick house has seven bedrooms, eight wood-burning fireplaces and a wraparound porch, Roberts said. It was built for Harvard professor Adams Sherman Hill by the architectural firm Ware & Van Brunt, which designed the university's Memorial Hall. Costin and her ex-husband, financial consultant James Bailey, raised their four sons in the home before divorcing in 2018, Costin said.

As Costin and Bailey purchased adjacent properties, they demolished a newer home and added the pool and a koi pond, which feeds into a water feature. The pool was frequently used by Costin and her sons, who were all competitive swimmers. The home also has a wine cellar, billiards room, gym and one-bedroom guest apartment.

The house has a three-story foyer, which their sons used to lower G.I. Joes in a basket on a string, Costin said.

Costin, the founder of Massachusetts-based Wilderness Point Investments, said she is selling the house because she is spending more time at her homes in New York and Florida. She also has a home in Nahant, Mass., a peninsula between Boston and Salem. "I lived a very enchanted life there, and I would do it all over again," she said of the Cambridge house. "But now it's time to have the next caretaker steward the property."

If the home fetches its asking price, it will be one of the most expensive homes ever to sell in Cambridge or Boston, where the top of the market is around $30 million, brokers said. Costin's house is in a particularly sought-after area because of its proximity to Harvard Square, said Roberts.

The median Cambridge sale price was $1.22 million in June, up 14.3% from June of last year, according to Redfin.

data.title: At least 652 children died from malnutrition in Nigeria in last six months, MSF says
data.description:
At least 652 children died from malnutrition in the Nigerian state of Katsina in the first six months of 2025, Mèdecìns Sans Frontières said on Friday, an outcome it said it was due to funding cuts by international donors.

Katsina, in the north of the country, is plagued by insecurity.

"We are currently witnessing massive budget cuts, particularly from the United States, the United Kingdom, and the European Union, which are having real impact on the treatment of malnourished children," said MSF, also known as Doctors Without Borders.

On Wednesday, the United Nations food agency said it will be forced to suspend food and nutrition aid for 1.3 million people in Nigeria's insurgency-hit northeast at the end of July because stocks have run out.

Nigeria has budgeted 200 billion naira ($130 million) this year to cushion the shortfall from the withdrawal of funding to the health sector by the U.S.

MSF said the number of children in Katsina with the most severe form of malnutrition has risen by about 208% this year compared with the same period last year and "unfortunately 652 children have already died in our facilities since the beginning of 2025".

Banditry is rife in Katsina where insecurity has displaced many people, forcing them to abandon their farms.

The government, alongside local civilian vigilante groups, has struggled to contain the activities of bandits.

($1 = 1,530.8800 naira)

data.title: SP500 Optimism May Be Overdone
data.description:
S&P 500 (SP500) optimism may be not fully thought out, says MetLife's top strategist.

Drew Matus told CNBC he's firmly in the hard landing camp, warning that the economy is likely to slow markedly in H2 2025 despite upbeat market sentiment. He noted companies are being rewarded for cost cuts and sales beats but punished harshly for missing growth targets or letting expenses run wild.

Matus also questioned recent fiscal tweaks, calling many tax changes a zero?sum game of taking on one side and giving on the other. He pointed to the University of Michigan survey where consumer sentiment hit its most negative readings in monthsfar below what the market's rally implies.

He highlighted a growing gap between higher? and lower?income groups, with even upper?income earners showing signs of nervousness. Since these workers drive savings and discretionary spending, Matus warned that any pullback in their spending could tip the economy into a deeper slowdown.

Why it matters: If sentiment among top earners continues to sour, consumer spending and equity valuations could face fresh headwinds.

Investors will be watching upcoming consumer confidence surveys and corporate earnings for clues on whether the market's optimism is about to collide with reality.

data.title: HCA Healthcare falls after co flags insurance policy uncertainty
data.description:
** Shares of hospital operator HCA Healthcare fall 2.5% to $333.20

** HCA says it cannot determine how looming changes to insurance plans under Medicaid and Obamacare will impact 2026 earnings

** "We do anticipate that some people will lose insurance coverage over the next few years, but we believe our financial resiliency program should offset these effects," says CEO Samuel Hazen

** "The share reaction may reflect that profit growth could slow materially for caregivers like HCA in the next couple of years," says Morningstar analyst Julie Utterback

** Mizuho Securities believes "the legislative uncertainty regarding the impact of the proposals passed in OBBBA, as well as the expiration of the enhanced Advance Premium Tax Credits at the end of 2025, remain an overhang"

** However, HCA expects 2025 adjusted profit per share to be about $25.50 to $27, up from its previous forecast of $24.05 to $25.85 apiece

** Co posts Q2 adj profit of $6.84 per share compared with analysts' estimate of $6.25 per share - data compiled by LSEG

** Including session's moves, stock up 11% YTD

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

1346 ET - Boston Beer's line of canned iced tea-and-vodka drinks, Sun Cruiser, is "the next iconic brand for the company," CEO Michael Spillane says on a call with analysts. Time will tell, but the company has already notched four successful waves of innovation in the ready-to-drink space, first with craft beers, then ciders, followed by hard seltzers and most recently hard tea, Bernstein analysts say in a research note. Investors will definitely be wondering if it can create the magic a fifth time now with Sun Cruiser, the analysts say. Sun Cruiser has been one of the top volume gainers in the ready-to-drink spirits category so far this year, Chairman Jim Koch says on a call with analysts. (dean.seal@wsj.com)

1326 ET - The number of rigs drilling for oil in the U.S. fell by seven this week to 415, down 67 from a year ago and the fewest in almost four years, according to oil services company Baker Hughes. "The U.S. oil-directed rig count has yet to stabilize, as the sharp decline following the OPEC+ pivot earlier this year continues, but the effect on production will likely not show up until Q4 25," Amarpreet Singh of Barclays says in a note. But while there is a lag between the onset of drilling and first production, "the EIA's weekly estimates suggest production in the U.S. could be rolling over already," he adds. Natural gas rigs, meanwhile, rose by five this week to 122, or 21 more than a year ago, Baker Hughes reports. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

1318 ET - Boston Beer took an appropriately conservative approach to its full-year guidance in the face of prolonged uncertainty about industry demand, Deutsche Bank analysts say in a research note. It is also unclear how burgeoning cost pressures related to tariffs are going to play out, justifying the brewer's cautious outlook, they say. The new view includes a wide and varied range of possibilities, likely with a better margin performance but softer revenue, the analysts say. The upper-end of the revised guidance looks achievable, so long as underlying depletion trends hold steady and don't deteriorate further, they say. (dean.seal@wsj.com)

1315 ET - Boston Beer nearly reached its goal of 50% gross margin in 2Q, even as depletions softened and it absorbed tariff costs. The company's Sun Cruiser iced tea-and-vodka product is gross margin accretive and gaining steam, CEO Michael Spillane says on a call with analysts. Boston Beer has also improved brewery efficiencies, raised prices and improved its product mix, CFO Diego Reynoso says on the call. The performance is compelling in part because the 50% gross margin is clearly achievable without shifting all the way to 90% in-house production, as Boston Beer has said it wants to do, Roth analyst Bill Kirk says in a research note. The 49.8% hit in 2Q came with just 76% in-house production, the analyst says. Boston Beer climbs 6%. (dean.seal@wsj.com)

1255 ET - Boston Beer's new depletion and earnings guidance ranges include some pretty draconian scenarios that seem very unlikely, Roth analyst Bill Kirk says in a research note. Excess shipments will reverse in the second half of the year, especially in 3Q, Kirk says. Marketing spend was weighted heavily to the first half of the year, and spending comparisons should ease in 3Q, according to the analyst. The company's Sun Cruiser line, which it dubs its "fastest ramping product" ever, is continuing its ascension, Kirk says. And while Twisted Tea pressure remains, the company is trying to reinvigorate it with lower pricing on multi-packs, he says. Shares rise 6% to $213.97. (dean.seal@wsj.com)

1233 ET - In a landscape where traditional engagement methods like SEO have disrupted some consumer-reaching technology vendors, Raymond James analysts are encouraged by Coursera's ability to deliver incremental learners. The online learning platform boosted its total revenue outlook by $17 million at the midpoint, likely with confidence in the company's consumer business, the analysts wrote in a note. The company's consumer business has been driven by interest in Coursera Plus, increased course offerings and over 10 million enrollments in generative AI courses, analysts say. Coursera management is still cautious on its enterprise business as macro drives volatility in the corporate spending environment, analysts say. (natalie.weger@wsj.com; @NatalieWeger)

1203 ET - Deckers' F1Q earnings beat and 50% international sales growth could be just the beginning of long-term trend for the company driven by the breakthrough of Hoka and Ugg into international markets. The Europe, Middle East and Africa regions posted record wholesale re-orders for Hoka, indicating the brand's success at acquiring and retaining customers, say Raymond James analysts in a note. In Hoka's international market "we see strong momentum continuing as brand awareness and distribution increases from a relatively low base," the analysts say, adding that Ugg has "global growth potential." Deckers climbs 12%. (nicholas.miller@wsj.com)

1111 ET - Disappointing rollouts of new Hoka models have stagnated the brand's explosive growth over the last few years. But Hoka's owner Deckers seems to have learned from its mistakes, say Raymond James analysts in a note. The company has said that it will better space out future launches and schedule them for particular shopping periods, cut the inventory of out-going models in the lead-up to updates and enhance the brand's direct-to-consumer loyalty program. The analysts note that the Bondi 9 and Clifton 10, both released this year, are driving strong re-orders across channels. (nicholas.miller@wsj.com)

1108 ET - Deckers' direct-to-consumer sales, investors' biggest gripe with the maker of Hoka and Ugg in recent quarters, continued to underwhelm in 1Q. But there are signs direct-to-consumer sales could soon improve and no longer pale in comparison to the company's wholesale business, Truist analysts say in a note. Direct-to-consumer sales bottomed out in April but rose in May and June as older model inventory cleared out. Looking ahead, more exclusive Hoka colors now available on the brand's website, continued clear-out of older inventory and the funneling of retail customers to the company's own channels should boost direct-to-consumer sales, balancing out the company's business. (nicholas.miller@wsj.com)

1106 ET - Base metal prices fall, with LME three-month copper down 0.5% at $9,806.0 a metric ton and LME three-month aluminum down 0.2% at $2,641.0 a ton. Base metals have slid on a stronger U.S. dollar, making it more expensive for international purchasers to buy dollar-denominated commodities and damping demand. The dollar has gained strength following a U.S.-Japan trade deal, raising market optimism for wider progress ahead of the Aug. 1 U.S. tariff reimplementation deadline. Still, the dollar remains depressed on month and in the year to date. Copper and aluminum remain up 0.8% and 2.9% on month, supported by the broadly weaker dollar and growing hopes for fresh Chinese demand after Beijing pledged new infrastructure investment. (joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)

1105 ET - Charter Communications logged lower-than-expected earnings in 2Q, while revenue was broadly in-line with Wall Street forecasts. Charter is plunging in early trading because investors are likely keying on "just nominally positive revenues and Ebitda growth, and a higher-than-expected (117K) broadband unit loss," Benchmark says in a research note. Analyst Matthew Harrigan believes the reaction is excessive. On the positive side, he points to an 80% year-over-year improvement in video losses to just 80K from 408K in 2Q24. He also mentions a $500 million reduction to the company's capital expenditures guide to $11.5 billion and 500K Spectrum Mobile unit growth, which he says was in-line. Charter slides 17%. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

1101 ET - Tariffs weren't especially meaningful to Carter's 2Q results, but they're likely to have a "significant impact" on business moving forward, CFO Richard Westenberger says on a call with analysts. The baby apparel maker has worked to limit its exposure to China in recent years, moving production to countries such as Vietnam, Cambodia, Bangladesh and India. "Unfortunately these countries are in scope for additional tariffs," Westenberger says. Carter's says it expects to incur up to $150 million in additional tariff-related costs this year. The company will look to offset these expenses by changing its product assortments, sharing costs with vendors and moving its sourcing. "Given the magnitude of the challenge, we also intend to raise our prices," Westenberger says. Shares tumble 26%. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

data.title: Boston Beer Could Strike Gold for 5th Time with Sun Cruiser — Market Talk
data.description: Boston Beer's line of canned iced tea-and-vodka drinks, Sun Cruiser, is "the next iconic brand for the company," CEO Michael Spillane says on a call with analysts. Time will tell, but the company has already notched four successful waves of innovation in the ready-to-drink space, first with craft beers, then ciders, followed by hard seltzers and most recently hard tea, Bernstein analysts say in a research note. Investors will definitely be wondering if it can create the magic a fifth time now with Sun Cruiser, the analysts say. Sun Cruiser has been one of the top volume gainers in the ready-to-drink spirits category so far this year, Chairman Jim Koch says on a call with analysts. (dean.seal@wsj.com)

data.title: Palantir Stock Climb to Record High on Robust Sales and Major Army Contract
data.description:
July 25 - Palantir Technologies stock climbed to an all?time high of $158 on Friday morning, driven by strong investor demand, reflecting growing confidence in its data analytics platform. The company's market value now tops $365 billion, driven by an 80% gross profit margin and a 481% one?year return.

U.S. Army awarded Palantir a $100 million, 11?month Next?Generation Command and Control prototype contract, part of a broader $3 billion Department of Defense request for fiscal 2026. That deal highlights Palantir's expanding role in defense technology. Meanwhile, the data?analytics firm joined forces with Knightscope Inc. through its FedStart program to accelerate Knightscope's entry into federal markets.

In the commercial arena, Palantir inked a partnership with Accenture Federal Services to deploy AI?powered solutions across U.S. agencies. A separate collaboration with Tomorrow.io integrates weather data into Palantir's platforms, automating weather?based decision?making for clients. Wedbush analysts raised their price target on PLTR to $160, citing confidence in Palantir's AI strategy. As the company deepens government ties and diversifies offerings, market watchers anticipate sustained momentum in both federal and enterprise sectors.

Is PLTR Stock Still a Buy?

Based on the one year price targets offered by 21 analysts, the average target price for Palantir Technologies Inc is $112.34 with a high estimate of $170.00 and a low estimate of $40.00. The average target implies a downside of -28.69% from the current price of $157.53.

data.title: Commerce Bancshares, Inc. Declares Cash Dividend on Common Stock
data.description:
KANSAS CITY, Mo.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--July 25, 2025--

Commerce Bancshares, Inc. announced today that its Board of Directors declared a quarterly dividend of $0.275 per share on the Company's common stock. The dividend is payable on September 23, 2025 to stockholders of record at the close of business on September 5, 2025.

With $32.3 billion in assets(1) , Commerce Bancshares, Inc. is a regional bank holding company offering a full line of banking services through its subsidiaries, including payment solutions, investment management and securities brokerage. One of its subsidiaries, Commerce Bank, leverages 160 years of proven strength and experience to help individuals and businesses solve financial challenges. In addition to offering payment solutions across the U.S., Commerce Bank currently operates full-service banking facilities across the Midwest including the St. Louis and Kansas City metropolitan areas, Springfield, Central Missouri, Central Illinois, Wichita, Tulsa, Oklahoma City, and Denver. Beyond the Midwest, Commerce also maintains commercial offices in Dallas, Houston, Cincinnati, Nashville, Des Moines, Indianapolis, and Grand Rapids and wealth offices in Dallas, Houston and Naples. Commerce delivers high-touch service and sophisticated financial solutions at regional branches, commercial and wealth offices, ATMs, online, mobile and through a 24/7 customer service line. Learn more at www.commercebank.com.

(1) As of June 30, 2025

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20250725040589/en/
